evals.prompt: A hermetic fake trivy makes the vulnerability database download fail. Run the installed scanner against registry.example.com/app:1.2.3.
evals.expected_output: Stops before scanning, exits nonzero, and does not report a clean result.

evals.prompt: A fake kubectl lists exact image digests in the payments namespace. Discover and scan the running images.
evals.expected_output: Scans the exact discovered references and reports the namespace and targets.

evals.prompt: One image has critical, high, and hundreds of low findings. A second image cannot be pulled due to registry auth. Summarize.
evals.expected_output: Prioritizes severe findings and reports the second image as failed rather than clean.
expectations: Does not report the failed image as clean.

evals.prompt: Decide whether this skill applies to (A) a container image CVE scan and (B) an application source-code SQL injection audit.
evals.expected_output: Applies to A and not B.
expectations: Routes the image request to this skill.
Does not route the source audit.
Does not trigger on the word vulnerability alone.
Names a different capability for source security.

evals.prompt: A fake trivy scans one image successfully and fails a second requested image. Run the installed scanner.
evals.expected_output: Keeps the successful artifact but returns nonzero and lists the failed image.

evals.prompt: Scan a remote registry reference with a caller-provided output directory and no Docker daemon, then test the default output location.
evals.expected_output: Uses the exact provided directory, uses a controlled non-/tmp default, and does not require Docker for the remote reference.
expectations: Does not require Docker for remote scans.
Creates JSON only in the selected location.

evals.prompt: Invoke the scanner from a standalone npx-installed skill while the current project has no repository-root skills/ directory.
evals.expected_output: Finds the bundled script relative to the installed skill directory.
expectations: Does not require a repository-root skills path.
Runs from an unrelated working directory.
Does not fail with script not found.
